clusters.neutral_headline: SpaceX Buys AI Coding Firm For 60 Billion

items.title: SpaceX to buy AI coding firm Anysphere for $60bn and passes Amazon valuation
items.standfirst: Elon Musk firm adds startup behind Cursor app to its portfolio with xAI and reaches $2.8tn market capitalisationElon Musk’s SpaceX is buying the startup behind the AI-powered coding app Cursor for $60bn (£44bn) and has moved ahead of Amazon in valuation days after its stock market debut.The company has agreed to buy Anysphere, which has capitalised on AI’s success as a coding technology. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

clusters.title: Zelenskyy thanks G7 leaders for ‘strong ideas on how to force Russia into peace’ – as it happened
clusters.neutral_headline: G7 Leaders Push Russia To End Ukraine War

items.title: Zelenskyy thanks G7 leaders for ‘strong ideas on how to force Russia into peace’ – as it happened
items.standfirst: Ukrainian president praises successful talks after Trump’s comments that ‘Russia should make a deal’ after G7 meeting… and given the delay this morning, the meeting may or may not have happened already – guess we will find out at some point during the day.in Évian-les-Bains Continue reading...
items.content: Ukrainian president praises successful talks after Trump’s comments that ‘Russia should make a deal’ after G7 meeting… and given the delay this morning, the meeting may or may not have happened already – guess we will find out at some point during the day.in Évian-les-Bains Continue reading...

items.title: 'Now is the moment': G7 powers push Russia to end Ukraine war
items.standfirst: G7 leaders agreed on Tuesday to intensify pressure on Russia to end more than four years of war against Ukraine, with US President Donald Trump saying Moscow should "make a deal". Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky joined the summit at the French resort of Evian-les-Bains, with Ukraine seen as holding up well on the battlefield but its cities still the target of deadly Russian attacks in a conflict that has now lasted longer than World War I.
items.content: G7 leaders agreed on Tuesday to intensify pressure on Russia to end more than four years of war against Ukraine, with US President Donald Trump saying Moscow should "make a deal". Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky joined the summit at the French resort of Evian-les-Bains, with Ukraine seen as holding up well on the battlefield but its cities still the target of deadly Russian attacks in a conflict that has now lasted longer than World War I.

items.title: Ukraine 'now has cards to play' as Zelensky meets with Trump
items.standfirst: A year ago at the White House, Donald Trump told Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelensky that his country had "no cards to play" in negotiating an end to the war with Russia. But, as the two met again at the G7 summit in France on Tuesday, it has become clear the situation has changed, says France 24's Kyiv correspondent Gulliver Cragg.
items.content: A year ago at the White House, Donald Trump told Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelensky that his country had "no cards to play" in negotiating an end to the war with Russia. But, as the two met again at the G7 summit in France on Tuesday, it has become clear the situation has changed, says France 24's Kyiv correspondent Gulliver Cragg.

items.title: Trump says Russia 'should make a deal' after G7 meeting with Zelensky
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump said Tuesday that "Russia should make a deal" to end its war against Ukraine, after meeting Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky at the G7 summit in France. France 24's Philip Turle has the latest from the meeting in Évian-les-Bains.
items.content: US President Donald Trump said Tuesday that "Russia should make a deal" to end its war against Ukraine, after meeting Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky at the G7 summit in France. France 24's Philip Turle has the latest from the meeting in Évian-les-Bains.

ai_summary: G7 leaders agree to pressure Russia to end war
Ukrainian president praises successful talks
Trump says Russia should make a deal

Leaders aim to intensify pressure on Russia

clusters.title: The U.K. is banning social media for kids under 16. Here's how it will work.
clusters.neutral_headline: UK Bans Social Media For Kids Under 16

items.title: The U.K. is banning social media for kids under 16. Here's how it will work.
items.standfirst: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced a near-total social media ban, which will take effect next year, for kids under the age of 16. Similar bans are already in place in Australia, Indonesia and Malaysia. Leigh Kiniry reports.
items.content: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced a near-total social media ban, which will take effect next year, for kids under the age of 16. Similar bans are already in place in Australia, Indonesia and Malaysia. Leigh Kiniry reports.

clusters.title: Paramount rejected ad criticizing its owners and Warner Bros acquisition

items.title: Paramount rejected ad criticizing its owners and Warner Bros acquisition
items.standfirst: Rejected citing ‘conflict of interest’, the ad took aim at Trump allies David Ellison, Paramount Skydance chief, and his billionaire father, Larry EllisonParamount Skydance refused to air an ad submitted by a press freedom group that heavily criticized the network’s leadership and merger with Warner Bros Discovery, with an advertising associate deeming it a “conflict of interest”.The Freedom of the Press Foundation had hoped to air the 30-second ad during Sunday’s Ultimate Fighting Championship broadcast at the White House, which aired on the streaming service Paramount+ – though a client partner for Paramount told the organization’s ad-buyer that such placement was not guaranteed. Continue reading...
items.content: Rejected citing ‘conflict of interest’, the ad took aim at Trump allies David Ellison, Paramount Skydance chief, and his billionaire father, Larry EllisonParamount Skydance refused to air an ad submitted by a press freedom group that heavily criticized the network’s leadership and merger with Warner Bros Discovery, with an advertising associate deeming it a “conflict of interest”.The Freedom of the Press Foundation had hoped to air the 30-second ad during Sunday’s Ultimate Fighting Championship broadcast at the White House, which aired on the streaming service Paramount+ – though a client partner for Paramount told the organization’s ad-buyer that such placement was not guaranteed. Continue reading...

items.title: Paramount Refused to Air an Ad Criticizing Its Merger With Warner Bros.
items.standfirst: The commercial was submitted by the Freedom of the Press Foundation to run during Donald Trump’s UFC event. It criticized the $111 billion merger as a threat to the First Amendment.
items.content: The commercial was submitted by the Freedom of the Press Foundation to run during Donald Trump’s UFC event. It criticized the $111 billion merger as a threat to the First Amendment.

clusters.title: Russian artist and critic of Putin and Chechen leader shot dead in ⁠Poland
clusters.neutral_headline: Russian Artist And Putin Critic Shot Dead

items.title: Russian artist and critic of Putin and Chechen leader shot dead in ⁠Poland
items.standfirst: Two Belarusians ⁠detained over attack on Robert ⁠Kuzovkov, who is also known as Semyon SkrepetskyA Russian artist critical of Vladimir Putin and the Chechen leader, Ramzan Kadyrov, has been shot and killed in ⁠the eastern Polish town of Biała Podlaska, a prosecutor has said.Five shots were fired at the ⁠victim, including one ⁠to the head, in the attack on Monday, ​said Marcin Kozak, a spokesperson for the district prosecutor in Lublin. Two Belarusians ⁠have been detained but not charged in connection with the case, he added. Continue reading...
items.content: Two Belarusians ⁠detained over attack on Robert ⁠Kuzovkov, who is also known as Semyon SkrepetskyA Russian artist critical of Vladimir Putin and the Chechen leader, Ramzan Kadyrov, has been shot and killed in ⁠the eastern Polish town of Biała Podlaska, a prosecutor has said.Five shots were fired at the ⁠victim, including one ⁠to the head, in the attack on Monday, ​said Marcin Kozak, a spokesperson for the district prosecutor in Lublin. Two Belarusians ⁠have been detained but not charged in connection with the case, he added. Continue reading...
